payload.name: SPACEX (proxy via SPCX)
payload.narrative:
SPCX is the publicly listed ticker for Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX), which completed its IPO in June 2026 at an approximately $1.75 trillion valuation — the largest IPO on record. The company is a vertically integrated space, connectivity, and AI enterprise: it designs and launches reusable rockets and spacecraft (Falcon, Starship), operates the Starlink satellite broadband constellation, and, following its acquisition of xAI in February 2026, develops frontier AI models (Grok) and runs the X social platform.

It sits on the watchlist as a space economy proxy because no other single listed entity combines launch services, satellite internet infrastructure, and frontier AI under one roof — making it a unique bellwether for the convergence of aerospace, telecom, and artificial intelligence. Its extreme concentration (one company spanning three sectors) and post-IPO price discovery phase warrant close observation of operational milestones — Starship cadence, Starlink revenue scale, and AI integration progress — rather than near-term trading setups.
catalysts: Quarterly earnings and forward guidance cadence
Sector-specific demand cycle and supply-chain signals
Macro regime shifts affecting risk appetite
